rationale: Directly matches every core requirement: shipped semantic search with sentence-transformers and FAISS at Google, built RAG pipeline using OpenAI embeddings + Pinecone at Amazon, trained XGBoost/LightGBM ranking models at Meesho (product company), and holds advanced Learning-to-Rank and LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning skills with tier_1 education from BITS Pilani and MIT.
Career is entirely marketing, sales, and accounting with a stint at fictional Wayne Enterprises; skills are PowerPoint, Angular, and Agile — zero ML, IR, or Python engineering experience.
Only 1.8 years of experience as a graphic designer and operations manager at Initech (a fictional company), with no ML, NLP, or search engineering background whatsoever.
Holds an IIT Kharagpur AI degree (tier_1) which signals technical foundation, but the entire 4.6-year career is content writing with no engineering or ML work delivered in production.
Honeypot profile with roles at fictional Dunder Mifflin and Initech, a career spanning only accounting, operations, and mechanical engineering, and no evidence of any ML or IR work.

Three separate roles listed at fictional Dunder Mifflin (graphic design, operations, mechanical engineering) and skills limited to Computer Vision, Excel, and HTML — no NLP, search, or ranking experience.

Career is entirely non-technical (accountant, graphic designer, civil engineer, operations manager) with a recent role at fictional Stark Industries, and skills are Marketing, Photoshop, and Accounting — no ML relevance.
Only 1.1 years of experience as a content writer at fictional Stark Industries, with no engineering or ML background and skills limited to content writing and basic tooling.

Solid 7-year data-engineering career at legitimate product companies (Swiggy, InMobi) with strong Python and pipeline skills, and RAG listed as an advanced skill, but self-describes ML as non-core and shows no evidence of vector-database, NDCG/ranking-evaluation, or production embedding-retrieval work.

Entire 10.6-year career is marketing, HR, and operations roles at IT-services firms (TCS, Wipro) with no engineering output; skills are PowerPoint and beginner-level cloud buzzwords with no ML substance.
